domain_experience: former compliance consultant for small financial firms
familiarity with annual review, exam prep, policy/process gaps, and documentation workflows
assets: domain pattern recognition in small-firm compliance workflows
ability to use AI to accelerate document triage, drafting, and issue logging
distribution: direct outreach to niche buyers or partner consultants
network_access: a few warm contacts in or around small financial firms
constraints: almost no desire to become a content creator
cannot justify a long software build before validation
credibility_signals: direct prior experience in the niche
knows the buyer language and recurring operational pain points
unfair_advantages: domain fluency plus warm access in a regulated trust-based niche
better judgment than generic AI-only operators about what is operationally useful versus legally sensitive

binding_constraints: The offer must stay on the operations, documentation, and preparation side unless the user is properly authorized for regulated advisory responsibilities.
The first offer must be fixed-scope and repeatable to fit 8 to 10 hours per week.
No reuse of prior employer or client confidential materials without clear rights.

opportunities.title: AI-assisted annual review and exam-prep package
opportunities.thesis: Sell a fixed-scope prep-and-evidence package that helps small financial firms assemble compliance review materials faster using AI internally for triage, checklists, and issue-log drafts.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user already understands the workflow, common gaps, and buyer language, and has warm access in a trust-sensitive niche.
opportunities.why_this_could_fail: The work may remain service-heavy and some buyers may really want licensed advice or sign-off rather than prep support.
validation_plan: Draft one fixed-scope offer sheet with explicit non-legal scope language.
Run 3 to 5 validation calls with warm contacts or warm-adjacent buyers.
Create one sample deliverable pack in under 3 hours.
kill_criteria: No repeated pain point after 5 validation calls.
Prospects consistently ask for services that require licensed sign-off the user cannot provide.

opportunities.title: Marketing and policy document pre-review support
opportunities.thesis: Offer a narrow operational review layer that flags inconsistencies, missing evidence, and follow-up questions before materials go to the actual approver.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: Regulated context matters and the user's niche knowledge improves review quality beyond generic AI output.
opportunities.why_this_could_fail: The scope can drift into substantive advice or approval unless tightly constrained.
validation_plan: Test whether buyers want pre-review support versus full advisory review.
Check whether the service can be framed clearly as preparation support.
kill_criteria: Buyer confusion around legal responsibility persists after revising the offer language.
The review workload cannot be completed within the user's weekly time budget.

opportunities.title: Subscription template and workflow library for compliance prep artifacts
opportunities.thesis: Package checklists, issue-log formats, evidence-request lists, and workflow packs into a recurring library for small firms or consultants.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user has the workflow knowledge needed to make the templates practical rather than generic.
opportunities.why_this_could_fail: Without service-led validation or stronger distribution, the asset may be low urgency and easy to copy.
validation_plan: Offer templates as part of pilots before selling them standalone.
Track which artifacts clients repeatedly ask to reuse.
kill_criteria: No client requests to reuse templates across pilots.
Templates require too much customization to become a true product.

opportunities.title: Compliance evidence concierge to standardized portal/workflow product
opportunities.thesis: Start with a fixed-fee service that collects, organizes, and drafts compliance prep artifacts, then convert the repeatable parts into a lightweight workflow product for evidence requests, issue logs, and policy cross-references.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: This directly matches the user's domain edge, warm access, and realistic time constraints while using AI to compress manual work in a high-trust niche.
opportunities.why_this_could_fail: The workflow may be too bespoke across clients, warm demand may be weaker than expected, or legal boundaries may limit how much of the process can be standardized.
validation_plan: Draft one offer sheet and one sample evidence pack on day 1.
List 12 warm or warm-adjacent prospects by day 2.
Complete at least 3 validation calls by day 5.
Create one concierge mockup in under 3 hours by day 6.
Make 2 paid pilot asks by day 7.
tool_specs: issue-log draft generation with confidence labels
kill_criteria: Fewer than 3 replies from 8 outreach messages.
Five validation calls produce no repeated workflow pain and no follow-up interest.
The first 3 pilots share less than 70 percent common workflow steps.
Buyers consistently interpret the offer as legal advice or licensed sign-off work the user cannot provide.

rejected_paths.title: Generic compliance AI copilot
rejected_paths.reason: Too weakly differentiated and too legally sensitive relative to the user's time and trust-based advantage.

rejected_paths.title: Pure content business
rejected_paths.reason: Conflicts with the user's stated preference and is unnecessary given warm-access validation routes.

rejected_paths.title: Broad fractional compliance consulting
rejected_paths.reason: Too bespoke for 8 to 10 hours per week unless narrowed into the recommended fixed-scope prep offer.

next_actions: Draft a one-page offer sheet with explicit legal scope boundaries.
Identify 12 warm or warm-adjacent contacts for validation.
Run 3 to 5 interviews focused on annual review or exam-prep workflow pain.
Build one sample evidence pack in under 3 hours.
Ask for 2 paid pilot conversations by the end of the first week.
